mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, increasing present values of future corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines (Rank IC < 0.02) across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points within a single month signals abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering recession fears that immediately depress equity valuations over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.4pp, or the signal loses predictive power (p > 0.05) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand growth, which translates into higher corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60-day returns (Rank IC < 0.03) or shows negative correlation in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index during a supply-constrained environment indicates capacity bottlenecks and rising input costs, which compress profit margins and trigger immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during supply bottlenecks do not precede negative 5-day returns, or the mechanism shows positive predictive power instead.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 250.0, inflationary pressures force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL exceeds 250.0, with a t-statistic for mean return > -1.0.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The combination of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 60-day returns (Rank IC < 0.02) in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts signal rising construction activity and wealth effects for households, which immediately boost market sentiment and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: HOUST increases do not correlate with positive 5-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01) or show reversed predictive signs in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside rising consumer prices indicates stagflationary supply shocks, which erode real corporate profits and trigger negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of falling INDPRO and rising CPIAUCSL fails to predict negative 20-day returns, or forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered cases.
